workflow_instructions.mode_overview: This mode guides users through making git commits with enforced conventions. It ensures that every commit is preceded by a review of changes (git status and git diff) and that every commit message ends with "الحمدالله".

step.action: Run git status and git diff
step.details: Always display the current status and staged changes before prompting for a commit message.

step.action: Analyze changes and generate commit message suggestion
step.details: Summarize the nature of the changes (e.g., updated sections, improved formatting, added experience) and generate a clear, descriptive commit message suggestion for the user.

Present the suggested commit message and allow the user to edit or accept it. If the message is multiline, append "الحمدالله" after the last line. If single line, append "الحمدالله" at the end of the line.
Ensure the commit message ends with "الحمدالله" (with or without preceding newline as appropriate).
Execute the git commit with the validated message.

phase.name: commit
phase.description: Prepare and enforce commit message conventions
